w:r.w:t: Students are allowed to use AI Tools:
This exam does NOT evaluate memorization of Flutter syntax.
Ability to understand and improve existing code
Students are free to use AI tools. However, students must be able to explain and justify their solutions.
You are a Flutter Developer working for an online smartphone store.
During development, the Product Owner continuously changes requirements.
You must build features, adapt to requirement changes, review source code, and justify your technical decisions.
Before writing any code, design the system architecture.
You may use any architecture pattern:
Why should API calls not be made directly inside Widgets?
If the API URL changes tomorrow, which layer should be affected?
If offline caching is required, where would you add it?
If the backend changes from REST to GraphQL, how would your design help?
Add your answer in this doc
PART B – BUILDING THE MVP (20 POINTS)
Using CircularProgressIndicator as the primary loading solution.
After completing the MVP, the Product Owner introduces the following changes.
Display only products that are in stock.
If stock < 10, display a red warning label.
Allow users to enable/disable stock warnings.
Is your current implementation still appropriate?
Propose at least two alternative solutions.
Add your answer in this doc
PART D – FAVORITE FEATURE (OFFLINE + UI CLONE) (10 POINTS)
Favorite data must persist after app restart
You are allowed to customize colors slightly
Why did you choose that solution?
How does your architecture support offline-first?
Add your answer in this doc
How data flows from UI → storage → UI
Add your answer in this doc
How would you detect this issue during code review?
Describe a test case that would reveal this bug.
Add your answer in this doc
PART F – OPEN-SOURCE PROJECT REVIEW (20 POINTS)
Clone and analyze the following Flutter project:
Students may focus on one sample application inside the repository.
Draw the architecture of the selected application.
Add your answer in this doc
PART G – AI AUDIT REPORT (5 POINTS)
How you corrected the AI output
Estimated percentage of AI-generated code vs. self-written code
Add your answer in this doc
Students may be asked any of the following questions:
How should timeout errors be handled?
How would you design an Offline-First application?
What happens when the number of items grows significantly?
Submit a ZIP file using the following naming convention:
AI tools are allowed without restriction.
Students must be able to explain every submitted solution.
Inability to explain submitted code may result in score deductions.
Reports must reflect the student's own analysis and reasoning.
The objective is not only to build software but also to evaluate, critique, and improve software engineering decisions.
